---
title: "Practice 2"
subtitle: "PUBH 523/623"
author: "Your name here"
date-modified: "today"
format: 
  html:
    link-external-newwindow: true
    toc: true
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    html-math-method: mathjax
    self-contained-math: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

## Overview

This practice is mostly **hands-on**. You will set up the real folder structure you will use for the rest of the course, create your R Project, write your first `.qmd` from scratch, and practice loading packages — all skills you will rely on every week.

Topics covered (Lessons 7–10):

- Folder and file organization
- Quarto and `.qmd` files
- Packages
- R Projects and working directories

## Directions

Practice assignments will follow a general format. Here are the following steps you need to follow to complete this assignment:

1.  [Download the practice `.qmd` from my GitHub repository.](https://github.com/nwakim/PUBH_523_26Su/blob/main/practice/practice_02.qmd)
2.  Save the `.qmd` file as `Lastname_Firstinitial_Practice_02.qmd` or `lastname_firstinitial_practice_02.qmd` (replace with your actual last name and first initial). Save it in your `practice/` folder with a `practice_02` subfolder if you'd like.
3.  Open the `.qmd` in RStudio (within your project) and edit it to complete the assignment. You will add your answers directly into this file.
4.  Keep the the boxes with the yellow stripe on the left side that explain the question.
5.  Do all your work under and outside of the boxes with the yellow stripe.
6.  You can delete other parts of this assignment, including:
    - Overview
    - Directions
    - Boxes that contain tips
    - Submission checklist
    - Note on AI usage
7.  Submit your completed practice to Brightspace as both a `.qmd` and `.html` file.
